steps.title: Set up the intersection equation
steps.content:
The graphs intersect where $x-c=-6(x-12)^2$. Expanding and moving everything to one side gives:
$$6x^2-143x+(864-c)=0$$

steps.title: Find the tangent boundary first
steps.content:
Two distinct real solutions require the discriminant to be positive. Use Desmos to find the boundary where the discriminant is $0$:
$$143^2-4(6)(864-c)=0$$
The regression returns $c=\frac{287}{24}\approx 11.96$.

steps.title: Pick the listed value that gives two intersections
steps.content: $c=\frac{287}{24}$ gives one repeated solution, not two distinct solutions. The value must be greater than that boundary. Among the choices, only $24$ is greater, so choice D is correct.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — '7': $7 < 287/24 \approx 11.96$, so discriminant $< 0$; no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice B — '11': $11 < 11.96$, so discriminant is still negative; no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{287}{24}$': Exactly at the threshold, discriminant $= 0$; only one (repeated) solution, not two distinct ones. ✗
  • Choice D — '24': $24 > 11.96$, so discriminant $> 0$; two distinct real solutions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
